questions.question: Based on paragraphs 10-12, what can you infer was important to Margaret as a photojournalist?

responses.content: the technical problems began. Black-and-white film at that time was sensitive to blue light, not the reds and oranges of hot steel. The pictures came out all black. Margaret solved this problem by bringing along a new style of flare (which produces white light) and having assistants hold them to light her scenes. Her abilities resulted in some of the best steel factory pictures of that era, and these earned her national attention.
responses.bucket_rationale:
Step 0: The only highlight to evaluate is: 'the technical problems began. Black-and-white film at that time was sensitive to blue light, not the reds and oranges of hot steel. The pictures came out all black. Margaret solved this problem by bringing along a new style of flare (which produces white light) and having assistants hold them to light her scenes. Her abilities resulted in some of the best steel factory pictures of that era, and these earned her national attention.'

Step 0C: 453 > 422. The student's highlight exceeds the character limit. Therefore, the answer is incorrect, regardless of content.
